bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself. The opponent’s opening offer is valid but only gives me moderate utility, and with no pattern of concessions yet I should anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for me than their first, so I do not read meaningful concession from their behavior; I will only concede on the low-weight issueD to avoid repeating my prior exact bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still very early (t=0.04), so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated essentially the same offer, which is low for me and not clearly improving, so I will make only a modest concession while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility, alternating only between about 0.40 and 0.44, with no clear upward concession trend; I will keep my high-weight issues at top values while conceding the lower-weight issueD to valueC, which they have repeatedly offered.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have not improved for my utility, staying around low-value combinations for me. I will continue targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on issueD, my least important issue, and a moderate movement on issueC rather than giving up high-weight issueA or issueB.

The opponent’s latest valid offer improved substantially for me by moving issueA to valueA, but it still asks me to concede on issueB and issueC, which are high-weight issues. Since it is still very early at t=0.10, I am targeting a high-utility bid while maintaining my prior low-cost concession on issueD to valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.877 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has recently moved issueA to my best value but continues to hold issueB/issueC/issueD near their preferred pattern, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while offering concessions on issueD and a partial concession on issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need to concede all the way to the opponent’s latest offer yet. The opponent’s offers have improved for me, especially their latest bid giving me my top values on issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issueC while keeping their repeated issueD=valueC concession.

I am targeting a high but slightly concessionary utility around 0.877, keeping my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while moving issueC from my top value to a compromise value. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB in the actual offer history but has consistently held issueC at valueC and issueD at valueC, so at this early time I will bridge on issueC without dropping all the way to their last position.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to concede down to the opponent's latest offer yet. The opponent has clearly conceded on issueA and issueB to my preferred values, while consistently holding issueC at valueC and issueD at valueC; since issueD is low weight for me, I accept valueC there, but I will continue pressing for my top issueC value for now.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 by holding firm on issueA and issueB, which are high-weight issues where the opponent has already conceded to my top values. Since it is still early at t=0.20, I am not yet willing to drop issueC all the way to valueC, but I will offer valueA there as a moderate concession while keeping issueD at valueC, which the opponent consistently proposes.

I am targeting a high-utility bid for me, about 0.961, by holding firm on the three high-weight issues while conceding the low-weight issueD to valueC. At t=0.22 there is still limited time pressure, and the opponent has already conceded on issueA and issueB but continues to push issueC: valueC, so I will not yet concede issueC that far. No messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.877 by holding firm on issueA and issueB, while conceding on low-weight issueD and partially conceding on issueC. The opponent has converged to issueA:valueA and issueB:valueA but keeps insisting on issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC; since it is still early at t=0.24, I will not yet drop issueC all the way to valueC, but I will offer valueA as a compromise instead of my top valueB.

At t=0.26, it is still early enough to target a high-utility bid rather than accept the opponent’s repeated issueC=valueC position. The opponent has conceded strongly on issueA and issueB toward my best values but is holding firm on issueC and issueD, so I will keep my high-weight issues at strong values while continuing to concede the low-weight issueD to valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility compromise around 0.877 by holding firm on the important issueA and issueB, while conceding low-weight issueD to valueC and offering issueC: valueA instead of my top valueB. The opponent has clearly conceded on issueA and issueB, repeatedly offering valueA there, but is holding issueC at valueC, so at t=0.28 I will not yet drop all the way to their issueC preference.

I am targeting a high but more conciliatory utility around 0.877, keeping my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while offering issueD=valueC, which the opponent has consistently proposed. The opponent has conceded on issueA and issueB but has held firm on issueC=valueC; since it is still only t=0.30, I will not fully concede issueC yet and will continue proposing issueC=valueA as a middle option.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.32, so there is no need to fully concede yet. The opponent has consistently conceded on issueA and issueB to my preferred values, but is holding firm on issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC; I will keep issueD conceded since it is low weight, while asking again for my best issueC value to test for movement without repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.88 by holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while making a moderate concession on issueC and keeping the lower-weight issueD at the opponent-favored valueC. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I do not need to concede all the way to the opponent’s repeated issueC:valueC position; their offers show they have already conceded on issueA and issueB but are holding steady on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while already conceding issueD to valueC. The opponent has consistently offered issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA recently but is holding issueC=valueC, so at t=0.36 I will continue pressing for my preferred issueC=valueB rather than concede further this early-mid stage.

I am targeting a high-utility compromise around 0.877: holding firm on issueA and issueB where the opponent has already conceded to my best values, while conceding issueD and partially conceding issueC from my top valueB to valueA. At t=0.38 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s repeated issueC:valueC demand, since that gives me much less on a high-weight issue; their offers show they are mostly fixed on issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC, with no messages adding credible new information.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 by holding firm on the three high-weight issues where my best values matter most, while already conceding issueD to valueC. The opponent has converged to issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA but keeps insisting on issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC; at t=0.40 there is still time, so I will not yet concede the high-weight issueC all the way to valueC.

I am targeting a high but somewhat concessive utility bid (about 0.877), keeping my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while moving issueC down from my top value. The opponent has repeatedly converged to issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA but is holding firm on issueC=valueC and issueD=valueC, so at t=0.42 I will test a middle concession on issueC while keeping issueD at their preferred/low-cost valueC.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.811 by holding firm on my high-value issueA and issueB while conceding issueC to the value the opponent has consistently demanded. At t=0.44 we are in the mid-game, and the opponent has repeatedly offered issueA:valueA and issueB:valueA but stayed fixed on issueC:valueC and issueD:valueC, with no messages to alter that read. This bid avoids repeating my previous exact offer and tests whether matching their persistent structure can close the deal while still beating reservation comfortably.
